methods.description: Process for categorizing gastropod shells into drilled and non-drilled groups to analyze predation patterns.

steps.description: Distinguish the drilled from non-drilled gastropod shells by counting the shells with one or more drillings visible.
steps.searchable_summary: categorize shells into drilled and non-drilled based on visible drillings

methods.description: Analysis of predation intensity determined as drilling frequency (DF) to estimate the frequency of interactions for a given taxon of prey.

steps.description: Calculate drilling frequency (DF) as the number of specimens of a taxon with one successful predation trace divided by the total number of specimens of that taxon.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate drilling frequency for each taxon
inputs: number of specimens with successful drill holes

methods.description: Estimate the predator's failure rate using the escalation parameter, referred to as prey effectiveness, documenting traces of unsuccessful attacks.

steps.description: Compute prey effectiveness (PE) as the number of trace fossils of predatory origin that record failed attacks divided by the total number of these trace fossils.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate prey effectiveness from trace fossils
inputs: number of incomplete and healed drill holes

methods.description: Categorize drill holes to indicate a probable predator, distinguishing between naticid, muricid, or undeterminable agents.

steps.description: Categorize drill holes as naticid (parabolic and countersunk) or muricid (cylindrical with straight edges) based on their descriptions.
steps.searchable_summary: identify predator type from drill hole characteristics
outputs: categorized drill holes by predator type
